title: Installation steps for WSL2 installs old version of Podman [3.4.2-1]
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

This is probably not a bug, but it is definitely not a feature; it is more of a question seeking guidance.
I selected bug because there are only 2 options (feature/bug) available.

**Description**


I am trying to move my local setup completely from Docker to Podman on WSL2, so trying to install latest Podman version on WSL2 using the kubic PPA

I am not able to install 4.0.x using the installation steps for WSL2. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Install using the steps from https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-windows-wsl2 ( Reached here by following links from https://podman.io/getting-started/installation )

```shell
. /etc/os-release
sudo sh -c "echo 'deb http://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/x${NAME}_${VERSION_ID}/ /' > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.list"
wget -nv https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable/x${NAME}_${VERSION_ID}/Release.key -O Release.key
sudo apt-key add - < Release.key
sudo apt-get update -qq
sudo apt-get -qq -y install podman
sudo mkdir -p /etc/containers
echo -e "[registries.search]\nregistries = ['docker.io', 'quay.io']" | sudo tee /etc/containers/registries.conf
```

**Describe the results you received:**

Podman 3.4.2-1 gets installed. 

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected latest package versions to be available in the PPA


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I tried checking https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_20.04/ and I see that only 3.4.2-1 package version is available here.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
❯ podman version
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 05:30:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
❯ sudo apt list podman
Listing... Done

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** 

WSL2 running Ubuntu Focal 20.04.04 LTS

```shell
❯ cat /etc/os-release

UBUNTU_CODENAME=focal
```

comments.body: @baude That's your article - might want to contact the Enable Sysadmin folks to request an update?
it should probably be retired ... agree @rhatdan ?

Podman 4.0 can use podman machine on windows, this should create a wsl instance with the latest fedora version AFAIK.

@n1hility Do we have a some docs/blog about this? I think the podman machine init page should be updated to reflect windows/wsl support.

@Luap99 Right now all we have is the WIP videos atm, which I agree isn't sufficient. We definitely need some overview doc. I agree machine init needs some notes (some of the config parameters are not applicable). To start I could throw together a basic Windows README or wiki page? I have two patches I am trying to squeeze in this week first (away next week)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@rhatdan Not yet but its next on my podman TODO after the volume stuff is done, so should get to it next week.
